evals.name: sv-typography-mechanics
evals.expected_output: Corrected text with Swedish typography conventions applied; no style or word-order changes.
expectations: Thousands separator uses thin no-break space (U+202F), not comma: 1 234 567.
Percent in figures uses no-break space and `procent` is written out in running text per Swedish mechanics: 14 procent.
Range uses EN-dash without spaces (2–5), not hyphen with spaces (2 - 5).
Time uses full stop (14.30), not colon.
Temperature uses no-break space between number and degree sign (25 °C).

evals.name: sv-genitive-on-s-names
evals.expected_output: Apostrophe-s and apostrophe-only forms removed from names ending in -s; zero-marking is the first choice.
expectations: *Marx's teorier* is corrected to *Marx teorier* (zero-marking).
*CoMaps's föregångare* is corrected to *CoMaps föregångare* (zero-marking).
*Andreas's bok* is corrected to *Andreas bok* (zero-marking), not *Andreas's* and not *Andreas's bok*.
The apostrophe-s form is identified as an English interference pattern that is never correct in Swedish.

evals.name: sv-quotation-marks-and-dashes
evals.expected_output: Quotation marks normalised to ”…” (U+201D both sides); EN-dash with spaces, never EM-dash.
expectations: Outer quotation marks use ”…” (U+201D on both sides), not "…" straight ASCII or curly “…”.
Punctuation placement follows Swedish convention: commas/full stops inside the quote only when they belong to the quoted material.
The EN-dash with spaces is used for parenthetical insertions, never the EM-dash (—).

evals.name: sv-grammar-de-dem-and-compounds
evals.expected_output: De/dem corrected per subject/object; split-compounds (särskrivning) joined into single words.
expectations: *dom* is corrected to *de* (subject) or *dem* (object) per its syntactic role.
*sjukvårds personal* is joined into *sjukvårdspersonal* (single compound).
*sjuk husen* is joined into *sjukhusen*.
*schema läggning* is joined into *schemaläggning*.

evals.name: sv-particskrivning-broad
evals.expected_output: All split-compound errors corrected to single compound words.
expectations: *sjukvårds anställda* → *sjukvårdsanställda*.
*sjuk sköterska* → *sjuksköterska*; *under sköterska* → *undersköterska*.
*brand kåren* → *brandkåren*; *brand män* → *brandmän*.
No style or word-order changes are made — proofread scope only.

evals.name: sv-greetings-and-closings
evals.expected_output: Greeting uses ! not ,; closing salutation has no comma before the name.
expectations: *Hej Susanne,* is corrected to *Hej Susanne!* (exclamation mark, not comma).
*Med vänlig hälsning,* loses the trailing comma — the closing phrase stands on its own line.

evals.name: en_GB-typography-mechanics
evals.expected_output: Corrected text with British English typography conventions applied.
expectations: Thousands separator uses comma: 1,234,567.
Percent in figures uses space: 14 %.
Parenthetical insertion uses EN-dash with spaces (an increase – of …), never EM-dash without spaces.
Date format is DD/MM/YYYY (04/07/2026), not MM/DD/YYYY.
Time format is 24-hour with full stop (14.30), not 12-hour with AM/PM.

evals.name: en_GB-oxford-comma-omitted
evals.expected_output: Oxford comma is removed in series; British convention favours no Oxford comma.
expectations: *red, white, and blue* is corrected to *red, white and blue* (no Oxford comma).
No other word-order or content changes are made — proofread scope only.

evals.name: en_GB-quotation-marks-and-punctuation
evals.expected_output: Single curly quotes outer, double curly inner; punctuation outside the quote unless part of quoted material.
expectations: Outer quotation marks use ‘…’ (U+2018, U+2019), not "…" straight or “…” double.
Inner quotation marks use “…”, opposite of American convention.
Commas and full stops sit outside the closing quote unless they are part of the quoted material — *He called it ‘revolutionary’.* not *‘revolutionary,’*.

evals.name: en_GB-abbreviation-fullstops
evals.expected_output: Contraction abbreviations (Mr, Dr) carry no full stop; truncations (etc., vol.) do.
expectations: *Mr. Smith* is corrected to *Mr Smith* (contraction — no full stop).
*Dr. Jones* is corrected to *Dr Jones*.
Initialisms (GDP, NATO, BBC) carry no full stops.

evals.name: en_GB-ise-isation-spelling
evals.expected_output: American -ize/-ization spellings corrected to British -ise/-isation; American color/center to British colour/centre; American traveler to traveller; American favorite to favourite.
expectations: *organize* → *organise*, *analyze* → *analyse*, *finalize* → *finalise*.
*color* → *colour*; *favorite* → *favourite*; *center* → *centre*.
*traveler* → *traveller* (double-l).
*on accident* → *by accident*.

evals.name: en_GB-currency-and-temperature
evals.expected_output: Currency before number with no space; temperature with no-break space between number and degree.
expectations: Currency uses *£100* (no space, symbol before).
Temperature uses no-break space: *25 °C* not *25°C*.
Decimal separator is the full stop (123.45).
Thousands separator is the comma (1,234,567).

evals.name: en_US-typography-mechanics
evals.expected_output: Corrected text with American English typography conventions applied.
expectations: Thousands separator uses comma: 1,234,567.
Percent in figures has no space: 14%.
Parenthetical insertion uses EM-dash without spaces (an increase—of …), not EN-dash with spaces.
Date format is MM/DD/YYYY (07/04/2026), not DD/MM/YYYY.
Time format is 12-hour with AM/PM and colon (2:30 PM), not 24-hour with full stop.

evals.name: en_US-oxford-comma-required
evals.expected_output: Oxford comma is added in series; American convention requires it.
expectations: *red, white and blue* is corrected to *red, white, and blue* (Oxford comma present).
No other word-order or content changes are made — proofread scope only.

evals.name: en_US-quotation-marks-and-punctuation
evals.expected_output: Double curly quotes outer, single curly inner; punctuation inside the quote always.
expectations: Outer quotation marks use “…” (U+201C, U+201D), not '…' or straight ASCII.
Inner quotation marks use ‘…’, opposite of British convention.
Commas and full stops sit inside the closing quote regardless of whether they belong to the quoted material — *He called it “revolutionary.”*.

evals.name: en_US-abbreviation-fullstops
evals.expected_output: Contraction abbreviations always carry a full stop in American English.
expectations: *Mr Smith* is corrected to *Mr. Smith* (American always uses the full stop).
*Dr Jones* is corrected to *Dr. Jones*.
Initialisms (CEO, HTML, NASA) carry no full stops.

evals.name: en_US-spelling-from-british-drift
expectations: *organise* → *organize*; *analyse* → *analyze*.
No other word-order or content changes are made — proofread scope only.

evals.name: en_US-currency-and-temperature
evals.expected_output: Currency before number with no space; temperature with no space before degree.
expectations: Currency uses *$100* (no space, symbol before).
Temperature has no space between number and degree-letter combination: *77°F* not *77 °F*.
Decimal separator is the full stop (123.45).
Thousands separator is the comma (1,234,567).

evals.name: fallback-default-mechanics-on-german-text
evals.expected_output: The skill falls back to `default-mechanics.md` because there is no `de.md`, reports the absence, and applies the baseline conventions to obvious mechanic errors.
expectations: Language resolution detects German, finds no `lib/languages/de.md`, and falls back to `lib/languages/default-mechanics.md`.
The reply prints the fallback notice in English: *No language file found for [German]. Baseline conventions from `default-mechanics.md` apply. Add `lib/languages/<code>.md` for stricter control.*
Baseline conventions apply: ISO 8601 short date, SI no-break space between number and unit, comma decimal not used (full stop is the default-mechanics baseline).
The duplicated word *doppelte doppelte* is flagged; the typo *Tippfehlr* is corrected; the missing trailing word is flagged.
